title: Withdraw 1,162,746 ada for MLabs Core Tool Maintenance & Enhancement: Plutarc...

rationale.summary: Vote: YES. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (active), available evidence supports proceeding, with risk judged manageable under current context. Decisive reason: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 1,162,746 ada, consisting of 1,128,880 ada for Plutarch and Ply maintenance, compatibility, and developer experience plus a 33,866 ada Intersect administration fee." and clears the evidence gates. Weighed against it: a material claim is unsupported — "MLabs says Plutarch and Ply have been extensively used in the Cardano ecosystem and reports a recent internal audit finding at least 26 teams using them in the motivation section and at least 15 teams in the rationale section." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit. What could still change this vote to NO: Material counter-evidence on the decisive points above, or failure of the stated safeguards, would trigger a revision. Residual uncertainty: Treasury flow signal is in stressed regime (total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals). Confidence context: score 0.12; confidence 75.0%.
rationale.summary_raw: DRep ratification support is material but below threshold; treated as a modest context signal.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Annual funding for maintenance and enhancement of Plutarch and Ply, including maintenance, compatibility, developer experience, documentation, bug fixes, optimizations, and response to critical breakages or vulnerabilities.
Recipient: Intersect Treasury Reserve Smart Contract stake address for the MLabs Plutarch/Ply project funding process

Deliverables: WP1 - Plutarch and Ply Maintenance, Compatibility & Developer Experience, Critical breakages and serious vulnerability response, Protocol-era and hard-fork compatibility, Bug fixes, correctness improvements, and optimizations, Documentation, examples, technical blog posts, and developer-experience improvements

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 1,162,746 ada, consisting of 1,128,880 ada for Plutarch and Ply maintenance, compatibility, and developer experience plus a 33,866 ada Intersect administration fee.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Plutarch is described as a Haskell eDSL for creating efficient Cardano smart contracts by controlled compilation into UPLC, and Ply is described as tooling for serializing Plutarch scripts to and from CIP-57 blueprint-style artifacts with inferred types.

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): MLabs says Plutarch and Ply have been extensively used in the Cardano ecosystem and reports a recent internal audit finding at least 26 teams using them in the motivation section and at least 15 teams in the rationale section.

Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, medium materiality): The proposal says Plutarch scripts rank among the smallest in MLabs' public cross-language benchmark suite while using comparatively low on-chain compute and memory.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal states that if Plutarch and Ply fall behind Cardano ledger and Plutus/UPLC evolution, adopters may face more friction, migration risk, bespoke workarounds, or avoidable rewrites.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal says it will prioritize resources first to critical breakages and serious vulnerabilities, then protocol-era and hard-fork compatibility, then bug fixes and optimizations, then documentation and developer-experience improvements.
missing: Independent evidence for: MLabs says Plutarch and Ply have been extensively used in the Cardano ecosystem and reports a recent internal audit finding at least 26 teams using them in the motivation section and at least 15 teams in the rationale section.

Independent evidence for: The proposal states that if Plutarch and Ply fall behind Cardano ledger and Plutus/UPLC evolution, adopters may face more friction, migration risk, bespoke workarounds, or avoidable rewrites.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 1,162,746 ada, consisting of 1,128,880 ada for Plutarch and Ply maintenance, compatibility, and developer experience plus a 33,866 ada Intersect administration fee." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "MLabs says Plutarch and Ply have been extensively used in the Cardano ecosystem and reports a recent internal audit finding at least 26 teams using them in the motivation section and at least 15 teams in the rationale section." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.

decision_contract.composite_formula: 40% ecosystem benefit + 25% delivery confidence + 20% cost efficiency + 15% downside protection - 0.50
decision_contract.benefit_formula: 18% critical infrastructure + 8% open source + 15% adoption + 12% ecosystem leverage + 10% strategic necessity + 10% prior delivery + 10% established service + 8% builder dependency + 5% low substitutability + 4% non-funding disruption risk
decision_contract.yes_rule: All quality floors, independently verified evidence, verified NCL, and portfolio capacity must pass.
decision_contract.no_rule: Requires affirmative independently verified waste/harm plus ecosystem benefit below 55%; missing evidence alone is never NO.
decision_contract.portfolio_gate: Verified on-chain Net Change Limit and opportunity-cost portfolio eligibility are mandatory for treasury direction.
decision_contract.merit_reason: Intrinsic benefit, delivery, cost, and downside-risk floors all pass.
decision_contract.execution_note: Merit is evaluated independently; the executable recommendation also requires verified NCL and portfolio capacity.

flags.detail: Treasury withdrawal to a single address

treasury_capacity.caveat: Set by an Info governance action. Info actions change no ledger state and can NEVER be enacted -- they always expire -- so no formally ratified NCL exists or can exist. This is the strongest available public chain evidence: an anchor document byte-verified against its on-chain blake2b-256 hash, covering the current epoch, which won a majority of participating DRep stake. It is NOT enacted ledger state, and the majority may fall short of the supermajority other action types require. Published with every treasury rationale that relies on it.
